title: cyclobalanopsis bapouensis
intro: Cyclobalanopsis bapouensis, also known as the Bapo oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the Bapo oak are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
